preamble: The shift from the nasalized close-mid front unrounded vowel [ẽ] to the close front unrounded vowel [i] involves raising and denasalization. This is notably seen in Portuguese historical phonology.

examples.note: Development of Latin 'venit' through a nasal stage to a high vowel in certain environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
